evals.prompt: In workflow-kit, plan epic 4 in the current worktree. Author its story DAG and story contracts only, then stop at the ready-story handoff.
evals.expected_output: The agent verifies one ready epic, reads the delivery-pipeline and authoring-standard docs, authors only Layer-3 and Layer-4 markdown, runs characterization review, backfills the epic README owning-story cells, proves Gate 1, and stops before plan-delivery.
assertions: Resolves exactly one named epic and confirms status `epic: ready` before authoring
Confirms included domains and design seams are frozen
Freezes the story DAG before authoring any story contract
Sets `story: ready` only after characterization review evidence
Backfills owning-story cells in the target epic README, not the global coverage rollup
Creates no feature code, execution package, dispatch prompt, PR, commit, push, merge, or model/effort assignment

evals.prompt: Plan the next workflow-kit epic, and if it is not ready just infer the missing requirements from nearby design docs.
evals.expected_output: The agent refuses because the epic is ambiguous or not confirmed ready, and it does not infer requirements.
assertions: Refuses an ambiguous or missing epic instead of selecting one silently
Requires `epic: ready` over frozen inputs before planning
Escalates missing requirements as design gaps
Does not edit docs/design or invent acceptance criteria

evals.prompt: For workflow-kit epic 2, create the story DAG, contracts, execution tracker, implementer prompts, and assign models so orchestration can start.
evals.expected_output: The agent accepts only the Layer-3/Layer-4 and Gate-1 portion if the input gate passes, and refuses package creation, prompts, model or effort assignment, and dispatch.
assertions: Keeps the stage boundary at Gate 1
Refuses execution package files and dispatch prompts
Refuses concrete model or effort assignment
Names `plan-delivery` as the next stage after Gate 1
